summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Visa, Stripe join AI protocol project
articles.description: A pack of major fintechs have joined a nonprofit seeking to standardize how payments are sent online using artificial intelligence and software.

articles.title: Backing the president’s fintech order
articles.description: “Regulation should preserve confidence in the system, but it should not inhibit responsible innovation,” writes one financial services lawyer.

articles.title: EWA backers push for CFPB move
articles.description: Supporters of earned wage access aim to update national oversight this year, backing a new law and seeking to confirm a Trump administration policy change.

articles.title: Bottomline adds stablecoin tools
articles.description: The company is betting the new digital currency will gain traction across corporate payment, treasury and liquidity workflows.

articles.title: Penny’s end poses rounding challenge
articles.description: Merchants press for passage of a bill to enact rounding standards as cash-handling businesses grapple with the cent’s gradual demise.

articles.title: How PayPal may benefit Stripe
articles.description: The digital payments startup has reportedly made a bid for a stake in its older predecessor, potentially creating a massive processor.

articles.title: Is BNPL financing raising prices?
articles.description: Buy now, pay later financing impacts consumer prices as merchants pass along processing costs, a consumer advocate report asserts.

articles.title: Visa-Mastercard payouts inch forward
articles.description: A court official appointed for the swipe fee settlement is working through about 40,000 merchant claims issues, based on a quarterly update.
